name: Sketchbook Saturday | Shreeya Sudhinndra
description:
We fill our sketchbooks with drawings of places, people, culture and heritage across India. Sketchbooks are private, and intimate space for artists to think, experiment, and try new ways of designing or creating art. Sketchbook Saturday is a bi-monthly event that sees artists from the Penciljam group sharing their sketchbooks and creative process with the broader creative community. Penciljam is a community that gets together on weekends to draw.
About Artist – Shreeya Sudhinndra
Shrreeya is a Bangalore-based 2D animator and motion graphics artist. She completed her BFA at Srishti Institute of Art, Design and Technology and a post graduation in Classical Animation from Vancouver Film School. She is deeply passionate about storytelling and finds joy in mark-making and the simple act of creating, often exploring art across different forms and mediums. You can find her on Instagram: @spills_quills.

BLR Design Centre in collaboration with Penciljam community presents “Sketchbook Saturday”.
Registration
Please register here to attend this event.  Please factor in your commute to arrive at the venue on time.

Please note: All individuals wishing to attend this event should register separately. If you need to cancel, please email blrdesigncentre@gmail.com so they can offer your spot to someone else.

name: Penciljam Bengaluru at Sabha
description:
The story of Sabha is rooted in a tradition of education and community. The building was once the Chaturveda Siddhantha Sabha School, established by the RBANM’s Educational Charities. For over a century, it was a place where generations of students were educated and where knowledge was shared. Today its a heritage structure featuring cultural events.
How to join this session
You don't need artistic skill to participate in this event - just boundless curiosity and a love for drawing. To connect with other participants, please use our community on Discord.
Once you arrive at the location, choose a spot you'd like to sketch and get comfortable.

Registration here for this event.
Things to carry for this session

 	A sketchbook/paper/drawing surface
 	Any medium you would like to use/learn/try.
 	A portable chair/mat. If the location has no seating, we tend to sit on the ground
 	Water bottle and snacks

name: Penciljam Bengaluru at NGMA
description:
Manikyavelu Mansion, once home to businessman-turned-aristocrat Vilum Manickavelu Mudaliar, was acquired by the government at an auction. Restored and repurposed, it became the southern centre of the National Gallery of Modern Art (NGMA) in 2009. Read more on NGMA here.
How to join this session
You don't need artistic skill to participate in this event - just boundless curiosity and a love for drawing. To connect with other participants, please use our community on Discord.

If this is your first time and would like future update on events, and more. Please register here.
Once you arrive at the location, choose a spot you'd like to sketch and get comfortable.


Rules at this location
No large easels are not allowed within the Galllery.
No wet media allowed inside the gallery/exhibit spaces
Be mindful to not block the walking paths.
Cafeteria is a billable space, so please order something if you choose to sit here.
Things to carry for this session

 	A sketchbook/paper/drawing surface
 	Pencils or any medium you would like to try out
 	A portable chair/mat. If the location has no seating, we tend to sit on the ground
 	Water bottle and snacks

How to get there
Follow the location map provided
Closest metro station: Vidhan Soudha (walk towards the Gallery, about 20 mins)
